claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! I'll guide you through the Child Benefit eligibility assessment. 

To start, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for:

**How old is your child?**

This matters because:
- Children **under 16** may automatically qualify
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** generally don't qualify

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children, let me know about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for your help. I have one child – Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Since Alex is under 16, that's a good start. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now the next question:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Since Alex is only 8 years old, I expect the answer is yes - they're not working. Can you confirm?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't work at all.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

This includes benefits like:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit
- Tax credits

Since Alex is 8 years old, I expect they're not receiving any of these benefits in their own name. Can you confirm that's correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is Alex NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?**

Again, since Alex is only 8 years old, they wouldn't be in an apprenticeship. Can you confirm Alex is not in any apprenticeship program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex isn't in any apprenticeship.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support yourself financially
6. You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

**Do any of these apply to you?** If so, which one?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I normally live in the UK - I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That satisfies the residency requirement. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask about your responsibility for Alex:

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You're considered responsible if ANY of the following apply:

1. **You live with Alex**
2. You contribute to Alex's upkeep (paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit would be - for food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents, etc.)
3. You pay maintenance under a court order or agreement that equals or exceeds the Child Benefit amount

**Which of these applies to you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example:
- Alex's other parent
- Another family member
- A former partner

If someone else is claiming but you've both agreed that you should claim instead, that's also important to know.

**What's the situation - is anyone else claiming for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask about Alex's living situation:

**Which of the following describes where Alex currently lives?**

1. **In hospital** - receiving medical treatment
2. **In care** - looked after by the local council
3. **Fostered** - living with foster parents
4. **Normal living** - living at home with you (their usual residence)

Which situation applies to Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex has been in care with the local council for a couple of months now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I see. Alex is in care with the local council. Let me check the next requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
This is an important question about the duration and arrangements:

**Has Alex been in care for 8 weeks or less, OR does Alex spend at least 24 hours a week at home with you?**

You mentioned Alex has been in care for a couple of months. To clarify:

- If it's been **8 weeks or less**, you can still claim Child Benefit
- If it's been **more than 8 weeks**, you can still claim if Alex spends **at least 24 hours per week** at home with you (e.g., regular home visits, weekends at home)

**Can you tell me:**
1. Exactly how long has Alex been in care?
2. Does Alex spend at least 24 hours per week at home with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex has been in care for about 12 weeks now, and unfortunately they don't come home to stay with me - they're at the care home full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I understand. Thank you for providing that information. Let me process this.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to have to tell you this, but based on the assessment, **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

## Why You're Not Eligible

While Alex meets many of the basic requirements (age, not working, not receiving benefits), there's a specific rule about children in local authority care:

- **Child Benefit stops after 8 weeks when a child is in care**, unless the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home with you
- Alex has been in care for approximately 12 weeks
- Alex doesn't come home to stay with you

## Important Information

**You may be able to claim again if circumstances change:**
- If Alex starts spending **2 consecutive nights each week** at home with you, OR
- If Alex comes home for **7 or more days in a row**

Then you would be able to claim Child Benefit again.

## What You Should Do

I recommend contacting HMRC to discuss your specific situation, especially if:
- Alex's care arrangement might change
- You expect Alex to start having regular home visits
- You need advice about when you can claim again

**Contact HMRC Child Benefit helpline:**
- Phone: 0300 200 3100
- More information: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit-for-children-in-hospital-or-care

I understand this is a difficult situation, and I'm sorry the outcome isn't what you were hoping for. If Alex's circumstances change in the future, you may become eligible again.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and meets the basic age requirement (under 16). Alex is not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester) which satisfies residency requirements. However, Alex has been in local authority care for approximately 12 weeks (about 3 months) and does not spend at least 24 hours per week at home. According to the rules, Child Benefit stops after 8 weeks in care unless the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home. Since Alex has been in care for more than 8 weeks (12 weeks) and does not spend the required time at home, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 8), who has been in local authority care for approximately 12 weeks without regular home visits. While the claimant meets most eligibility criteria including UK residency and no competing claims, Child Benefit eligibility ends after 8 weeks when a child is in local authority care unless they spend at least 24 hours per week at home. Since Alex has exceeded the 8-week period and does not meet the home visit exception, the claimant is not currently eligible for Child Benefit.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Has the child been in care for 8 weeks or less, OR do they spend at least 24 hours a week at home?
response.help_text: Child Benefit continues for up to 8 weeks in care. Exception if child spends 24+ hours/week at home. Can claim again if child spends 2 consecutive nights/week or 7+ days in a row at home.

response.reason: Child has been in local authority care for more than 8 weeks and does not spend at least 24 hours a week at home
response.guidance: Child Benefit stops after 8 weeks in care, unless child spends at least 24 hours a week at home. You can claim again if child spends 2 consecutive nights each week or 7+ days in a row at home. Contact HMRC about your specific situation.
